equivalence_argument: No complete mapping: metrics/RPC types can carry demotions, but there is no BlockBuildAttemptRecorder-style producer that snapshots demotions on better_payload, aborted_not_better, or cancelled exits.

negative_search_evidence.missing_behavior: Payload build attempts do not snapshot policy demotions before success, abort, or cancellation outcomes, because the attempt recorder/producer path is absent.
related_but_insufficient_code: crates/rpc/rpc-api/src/ritual.rs exposes demotions in the debug response shape.
Search found no BlockBuildAttemptRecorder, record_demotions, or payload-builder call to record_block_build_attempt.

notes: This aligns with the adjacent block-build trace producer gap.
rationale: The commit adds demotions to BlockBuildAttemptRecorder, record_demotions converts policy events to metric traces when enabled, Drop passes demotions to the metrics ring buffer, and all shown cancellation/abort/success paths call record_demotions. No candidate in this bounded assignment contradicts or removes it.
statement: When block building exits as better_payload, aborted_not_better, or cancelled, BlockBuildAttemptRecorder must record the current policy demotions before setting the final outcome and flushing the attempt trace on drop.
suggested_falsification_tests: Force payload building through cancelled, aborted_not_better, and better_payload outcomes after at least one policy demotion and assert the emitted build attempt trace includes the demotion work_class, reason, and elapsed_micros for each outcome.
summary: Build attempts do not flush demotions on terminal outcomes.
test_or_falsification_candidate: Force cancelled, not-better, and better-payload exits after a demotion and assert ritual_getBlockBuildTrace contains the demotion for each; current producer wiring is absent.
title: Build attempts flush demotions on every outcome
